title: for loop increment clauses should modify the loops' counters
description: "for" loop increment clauses should modify the loops' countersAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoTheforloop is designed to iterate over a range using a counter variable, with the counter being updated in the loop’s increment section. Misusing this structure can lead to issues such as infinite loops if the counter is not updated correctly. If this is intentional, use awhileordo whileloop instead of aforloop.Using a for loop for purposes other than its intended use can lead to confusion and potential bugs.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects for loops where increment clause doesn't modify the loop counter (wrong variable incremented or i += 0)
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when: ForStatement increment clause references different variable than counter OR increments by zero
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes: comments, test code, intentional patterns with explanatory comments
logic.reasoning: For loops should increment their declared counter variable to avoid infinite loops or confusion. This rule detects two anti-patterns: 1) Increment clause modifying a different variable than the loop counter (e.g., 'for (int i = 0; i < 10; j++)'), and 2) Increment by zero (e.g., 'i += 0'). Pattern matches for loop structure and extracts counter variable name from initialization, then checks if increment clause uses the same variable or adds zero.
